rulings.Case Title: DODEKA, LLC VS. JEREMIAH TURNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2012, Line 21. Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Hearing required. If the judgment debtor can provide documentary evidence (such as a bank statement for the account at issue) tracing the amount levied upon to paid earnings, it is exempt in the amount provided for in CCP 704.070. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHASE BANK USA, N.A. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition To Appoint Arbitrator Or Issue A Ruling That Arbitration Is Not Required And The Matter May Be Litigatedq
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2012, Line 3. RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDINER'S Petition To Appoint Arbitrator Or Issue A Ruling That Arbitration Is Not Required And The Matter May Be Litigated. Denied. The subject correspondence sent on May 24, 2012 is a settlement offer and predates the court order confirming the arbitration award. Respondent's additional claim for violation of banking privacy laws is outside the scope of this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PRIME LASALLE/MADISON PARTNERS LLC VS. GRIGSBY & ASSOCIATES INC. AKA GRIGSBY & ASSOCIATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Of Rights And Restraining Disposition Of Right To Payment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF PRIME LASALLE/MADISON PARTNERS LLC Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Of Rights And Restraining Disposition Of Right To Payment Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jw

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE SCOTT VS. HEALTH NET OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, And Special Interrogatories, Sets One; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF BONNIE SCOTT Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, And Special Interrogatories, Sets One; And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel are requested to appear and argue. The court is not satisfied that the parties have fully met their obligation to meet and confer in good faith. At the hearing, counsel for the parties shall advise the court of the progress they have made to resolve this matter. The court will refrain from issuing a tentative ruling until after the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jw

rulings.Case Title: SUPERIOR STONE PRODUCTS, INC. VS. BLADE RUNNER TOOLS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2012, Line 15. DEFENDANTS BLADE RUNNER TOOLS, INC., SHADY ALZAYAT'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action. A plaintiff need not elect, and cannot be compelled to elect, between inconsistent remedies during the course of trial prior to judgment. (See Baker v. Superior Court (1983) 150 Cal.App.3d 140, 145.) Sustained with leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead whether the agreement breached is oral or written. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
